data.title: Farmers National Banc Reports Strong First-Quarter 2026 Results
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Farmers National Banc Oh ( (FMNB) ) has provided an announcement.

Farmers National Banc Corp., a community-focused financial services provider in Ohio and Pennsylvania, expanded its scale to $7.18 billion in assets by March 31, 2026, driven largely by its acquisition of Middlefield Banc Corp. on March 2, 2026. The combination added significant loans, deposits and equity, while slightly elevating nonperforming loans, and is intended to deepen Farmers’ competitive position in its regional markets, especially Columbus.

On April 22, 2026, Farmers reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $16.3 million, or $0.36 per diluted share, which rose to $20.0 million, or $0.45 per share, on an adjusted basis excluding $4.0 million of Middlefield acquisition and core conversion costs. The quarter marked the company’s 173rd consecutive period of profitability, featured higher net interest income and margin, stronger noninterest revenue lines and modest credit cost recoveries, offset by higher operating expenses tied to integration and technology conversion efforts that management expects to complete in the third quarter of 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (FMNB) stock is a Buy with a $15.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Farmers National Banc Oh stock, see the FMNB Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FMNB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FMNB is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid financial performance (improving margins, healthier leverage trend, and strong 2025 free cash flow) and an attractive valuation (low P/E with a steady dividend). Technicals are supportive with price above key averages and positive momentum, while corporate events add upside via merger progress and leadership additions tempered by potential dilution from the share authorization increase.

To see Spark’s full report on FMNB stock, click here.

More about Farmers National Banc Oh

Farmers National Banc Corp. is a diversified financial services company headquartered in Canfield, Ohio, operating primarily through The Farmers National Bank of Canfield. The bank focuses on community banking and wealth management services across its core Ohio and Pennsylvania markets, with a growing presence in Columbus supported by recent investments and acquisitions.

An announcement from DeFi Technologies ( (TSE:DEFI) ) is now available.

On April 22, 2026, DeFi Technologies said its asset management arm Valour secured about US$11 million in institutional investments into its Hedera (HBAR) exchange-traded products, with US$10 million allocated on Börse Frankfurt and US$1 million on Sweden’s Spotlight Exchange. The deals, driven by investors’ bullish stance on Hedera and originated via the company’s Abu Dhabi Insights Symposium, mark a key step in growing assets under management in Germany and solidifying Börse Frankfurt as a strategic venue.

The company highlighted that the transactions underscore rising institutional demand for regulated digital asset exposure and demonstrate the signaling value of its DEFT Valour Investment Opportunity Index, which tracks flows across its ETP suite. Management said higher AUM directly boosts Valour’s fee-based revenues and operating leverage, while the inflows enhance Hedera’s institutional profile and reinforce DeFi Technologies’ positioning at the intersection of traditional finance and digital assets through data-driven product development and investor outreach.

data.title: Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals to Showcase EP-104GI Data at Digestive Disease Week 2026
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals ( (TSE:EPRX) ) has provided an update.

Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals, a clinical-stage biotech specializing in locally delivered, extended-release drugs built on its Diffusphere technology platform, is advancing candidates such as EP-104GI for eosinophilic esophagitis and EP-104IAR for knee osteoarthritis pain, with broader ambitions in gastrointestinal, joint and oncology indications. The company, which previously reported that its Phase 2b SPRINGBOARD trial of EP-104IAR met its primary endpoint, continues to build a diversified pipeline of long-acting formulations aimed at improving safety, tolerability and durability for approved and novel therapies.

On April 22, 2026, Eupraxia announced it will present four abstracts at the Digestive Disease Week annual meeting in Chicago from May 2–5, 2026, highlighting clinical data from its ongoing Phase 1b/2 RESOLVE trial of EP-104GI in eosinophilic esophagitis. The presentations will cover dose escalation, durability of histologic, endoscopic and dysphagia improvements, and optimization of dose and injection patterns, while a separate virtual investor event at DDW on May 4, 2026, will showcase key opinion leaders and emphasize EP-104GI’s potential to prevent esophageal strictures, underscoring the program’s strategic importance and visibility with both clinicians and investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:EPRX) stock is a Buy with a C$11.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals stock, see the TSE:EPRX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on EPRX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, EPRX is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by the pre-revenue model with widening losses and rising cash burn, despite a notably improved, low-debt balance sheet. Technical indicators also reflect weak near-term momentum, and valuation signals are limited by negative earnings and lack of dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on EPRX stock, click here.

More about Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals

Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on locally delivered, extended-release therapies targeting areas of high unmet medical need, particularly pain and inflammatory gastrointestinal diseases. Its proprietary Diffusphere micro-sphere technology is designed for hyper-local, prolonged drug delivery, and its pipeline includes EP-104GI for eosinophilic esophagitis and EP-104IAR for knee osteoarthritis pain, alongside potential programs in oncology and other inflammatory indications.

Evaxion Biotech ( (EVAX) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 16, 2026, Evaxion A/S held its Annual General Meeting, where shareholders representing 25.15% of outstanding ordinary shares approved the 2025 audited annual report and resolved that the year’s loss be carried forward. The meeting re-elected four directors, appointed Jens Bitsch-Nørhave as a new board member, and confirmed EY Godkendt Revisionspartnerselskab as auditor, reinforcing continuity in governance and oversight.

Shareholders also approved amendments to Evaxion’s Articles of Association to expand the board’s authority to issue up to DKK 11.9 million in warrants and to establish a new authorization to raise up to DKK 73.5 million via convertible loan notes, both without pre-emptive rights for existing shareholders. These changes, effective April 16, 2026, provide the company with greater flexibility to incentivize key personnel and access equity-linked financing, potentially supporting future growth but also opening the door to share dilution for current investors.

data.title: Spire Divests Mississippi Gas Utility to Refocus Footprint
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Spire ( (SR) ) has shared an update.

On April 21, 2026, Spire’s subsidiary Spire EnergySouth agreed to sell all shares of Spire Mississippi Inc., its Mississippi natural gas utility serving about 18,000 customers via roughly 745 miles of pipelines, to Delta Mississippi Gas Company, an affiliate of Delta Utilities, for $75 million in cash. The deal, announced publicly on April 22, 2026, values the business at 1.4 times its 2025 rate base and is expected to close in the first quarter of Spire’s fiscal 2027, subject to approval by the Mississippi Public Service Commission and other customary conditions.

Spire plans to use the sale proceeds to fund infrastructure investments at its larger regulated gas utilities in Alabama, Missouri and Tennessee, refining its geographic footprint and sharpening its focus on bigger regulated markets. The agreement includes standard termination rights, a reverse termination fee of $7.5 million payable by the buyer under certain conditions, and the planned transfer of frontline employees to Delta to support continuity of service, signaling a strategic portfolio realignment for Spire and an expansion of Delta’s Mississippi presence.

The most recent analyst rating on (SR) stock is a Buy with a $102.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Spire stock, see the SR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SR is a Neutral.

The score is mainly constrained by weak free cash flow and leverage overhang in the annual balance sheet data, despite improving profitability. Offsetting that, the stock shows a constructive technical uptrend, valuation/dividend are supportive, and the latest guidance and strategic actions (Tennessee expansion and storage divestiture) are broadly positive but carry execution and financing-related risks.

To see Spark’s full report on SR stock, click here.

More about Spire

Spire Inc., listed on the NYSE as SR, is one of the largest publicly traded natural gas companies in the U.S., serving nearly 2 million homes and businesses. Its core operations include regulated gas utilities in Alabama, Mississippi, Missouri and Tennessee, alongside related businesses Spire Marketing and Spire Midstream focused on natural gas services and infrastructure.

Delta Utilities, backed by Bernhard Capital Partners, ranks among the nation’s top 40 natural gas providers with about 600,000 customers across Louisiana and Mississippi. Headquartered in New Orleans, it employs 875 people and emphasizes investment to deliver clean, safe and affordable natural gas with a strong focus on service reliability and safety.

data.title: TuHURA Biosciences Secures $50 Million Credit Facility
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

TuHURA Biosciences ( (HURA) ) has provided an update.

On April 21, 2026, TuHURA Biosciences, Inc. entered into a secured loan agreement with Parkview Holdings One LLC, an affiliate of its largest shareholder, establishing a revolving credit facility of up to $50 million maturing on April 21, 2031 at a 12% annual interest rate. The facility allows monthly draws tied to TuHURA’s expense budget and is expected to extend the company’s cash runway into early 2028 while funding ongoing clinical trials and development programs, including its Phase 3 IFx-2.0 program and TBS-2025.

As part of the transaction, TuHURA agreed to a $5 million commitment fee payable in stock subject to shareholder approval, an annual 1.5% facility fee, profit-based principal repayments once product sales generate consecutive quarterly net profits, and restrictive covenants on additional debt, investments, and certain corporate actions. The company also amended warrants held by the shareholder affiliate to extend their exercise period to 2031 and granted Parkview a low to mid-single digit royalty on future net sales of IFx-2.0–based products, reinforcing insider backing but adding leverage, asset liens, and royalty obligations that will shape the economics of any future commercialization.

The most recent analyst rating on (HURA) stock is a Buy with a $7.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on TuHURA Biosciences stock, see the HURA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HURA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HURA is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial fundamentals—no revenue, widening losses, and sharply worsening operating cash flow—implying continued reliance on financing. Technicals are a relative positive with price trending above major moving averages, but momentum is stretched (high RSI/Stoch). Corporate-event risk is elevated due to the Nasdaq minimum bid price notice, while valuation is difficult to support given negative earnings and no indicated dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on HURA stock, click here.

More about TuHURA Biosciences

TuHURA Biosciences, Inc. is a Phase 3 immuno-oncology company developing novel therapeutics to overcome primary and acquired resistance to cancer immunotherapy in patients with advanced cancers. Its lead innate immune agonist IFx-2.0 is in a randomized Phase 3 registration trial as an adjunct to Keytruda for first-line treatment of advanced or metastatic Merkel cell carcinoma.

The company’s pipeline also includes TBS-2025, a VISTA-inhibiting monoclonal antibody moving into Phase 1b/2 development in mutNPM1 relapsed/refractory AML, and Delta Opioid Receptor-based bi-specific ADCs targeting myeloid-derived suppressor cells. TuHURA is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker HURA and focuses on addressing resistance mechanisms that limit the durability and efficacy of checkpoint inhibitors and cellular therapies.

Average Trading Volume: 1,954,375

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $179.9M

data.title: Rafael Holdings expands Alzheimer’s portfolio with MIT license
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rafael Holdings ( (RFL) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 22, 2026, Rafael Holdings said its subsidiary Cyclo Therapeutics signed an exclusive license with MIT for a U.S. patent covering the use of cyclodextrins in treating Alzheimer’s patients who carry the ApoE4 mutation. The deal expands protection around Trappsol® Cyclo™ in an ApoE4-positive population, where dysregulated cholesterol metabolism is thought to drive amyloid and tau pathology.

Rafael highlighted preclinical data indicating Trappsol® Cyclo™ can improve cholesterol transport between central nervous system cells, reduce tau pathology and enhance learning and memory in mice, potentially targeting underlying disease mechanisms. Executives framed the license as a transformative milestone that strengthens the company’s Alzheimer’s program while it awaits Phase 3 results in Q3 2026 from Transport NPC, the largest study conducted in Niemann-Pick Type C1, which could further shape Trappsol® Cyclo™’s role across multiple indications.

Spark’s Take on RFL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RFL is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by weak financial performance driven by large ongoing losses and cash burn, partially offset by low leverage. Technical indicators are mixed/neutral, and valuation is not supportive given negative earnings and no dividend data.

To see Spark’s full report on RFL stock, click here.

More about Rafael Holdings

Rafael Holdings, Inc. is a biotechnology company that develops pharmaceuticals and holds interests in clinical and early-stage companies focused on drugs and medical devices. Its lead candidate, Trappsol® Cyclo™, is being evaluated in a pivotal Phase 3 clinical trial for the treatment of Niemann-Pick Disease Type C1, a rare, fatal and progressive genetic disorder.

The company also maintains stakes in other clinical-stage and early-stage pharmaceutical developers as well as an orthopedic-focused medical device firm, reflecting a diversified portfolio in life sciences. Rafael’s strategy centers on advancing Trappsol® Cyclo™ while leveraging its broader holdings to participate in multiple therapeutic areas and technologies.

data.title: Best Buy Announces CEO Succession and Leadership Transition
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Best Buy Co ( (BBY) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 22, 2026, Best Buy announced a CEO succession plan under which current Chief Executive Officer Corie Barry will step down from her CEO and board roles at the end of the day on Oct. 31, 2026, after seven years at the helm. Jason Bonfig, currently Senior Executive Vice President overseeing customer offering, fulfillment and Best Buy Canada, was appointed CEO and director effective Nov. 1, 2026, becoming the sixth chief executive in the company’s 60-year history.

Bonfig’s promotion follows an extensive internal and external search and underscores Best Buy’s emphasis on continuity in merchandising, e-commerce, marketing, supply chain and its retail media network, Best Buy Ads, as well as growth initiatives such as its U.S. online Marketplace. Under a new employment agreement, his compensation will rise materially, including a higher base salary, a larger short-term incentive target and increased long-term equity awards, reflecting the Board’s confidence in his ability to drive growth and create shareholder value.

The company also detailed severance protections for Bonfig, including eligibility for enhanced severance in the event of termination without cause or for good reason within 12 months of a change in control, aligning his package with market norms for large U.S. retailers. Barry will remain with Best Buy for six months after her Oct. 31, 2026, departure as a strategic advisor, at a reduced base salary but with continued executive-level benefits, to support a smooth leadership transition and preserve stability for employees, partners and investors.

Board Chair David Kenny cited Barry’s steady leadership through tumultuous periods and praised Bonfig’s track record in building key growth platforms, while Barry publicly endorsed Bonfig as the right leader to accelerate the company’s strategy. The orderly, overlapping transition structure and board-level continuity signal that Best Buy intends to maintain strategic momentum in core consumer electronics, digital commerce and services while minimizing disruption to its operations and stakeholder relationships.

The most recent analyst rating on (BBY) stock is a Hold with a $65.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Best Buy Co stock, see the BBY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BBY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BBY is a Neutral.

BBY scores 51 mainly due to pressured financial performance (sharp TTM revenue decline and thinner profitability) and weak technical setup (below key moving averages with negative momentum). The high dividend supports valuation, and the latest call points to modest gross margin tailwinds from ads/marketplace, but guidance implies continued near-term margin pressure and elevated uncertainty.

To see Spark’s full report on BBY stock, click here.

More about Best Buy Co

Best Buy Co., Inc. is the world’s largest specialty consumer electronics retailer, offering a curated assortment of name-brand technology products and related services across stores, online channels and in customers’ homes. The company focuses on enriching lives through technology, operating more than 1,000 stores in North America, employing over 80,000 people and generating $41.7 billion in fiscal 2026 revenue.

Best Buy provides expert advice, design, delivery, installation, tech support and repair services that support its circular-economy initiatives, including repair, trade-in and recycling programs. It is also a recognized leader in corporate responsibility and sustainability, backed by the Best Buy Foundation’s Teen Tech Center network, which expands access to technology and skills for young people across the U.S.

data.title: Certara to Divest Regulatory and Medical Writing Business
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Certara ( (CERT) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 21–22, 2026, Certara entered a definitive agreement to sell its global Regulatory and Medical Writing business, including related subsidiaries and assets, to Veristat for up to $135 million in cash, comprising $100 million upfront and up to $35 million in earn‑outs. The deal, expected to close in the second quarter of 2026 subject to customary conditions and possible escrow of up to $15 million, involves a unit that generated $50 million in 2025 revenue, $17 million in adjusted EBITDA and employs about 220 staff.

Management said the divestiture will sharpen Certara’s focus on its core model‑informed drug development and Clinical Intelligence solutions and support accelerated investment in its integrated MIDD platform and product innovation. The move signals a strategic pivot away from services‑heavy regulatory writing work toward higher‑margin, AI‑integrated software and analytics, with the company planning to update its 2026 guidance once the transaction closes, an event that could reshape its growth profile and capital allocation priorities for customers and shareholders.

More about Certara

Certara, Inc., listed on Nasdaq as CERT, is a global leader in model‑informed drug development that accelerates medicines using biosimulation software, technology and services. The company serves more than 2,600 biopharmaceutical companies, academic institutions and regulatory agencies across 70 countries, focusing on data‑driven solutions across the drug discovery and development lifecycle.

Certara’s portfolio centers on model‑informed drug development and Clinical Intelligence platforms, which are increasingly being integrated with AI‑driven modeling and simulation. By concentrating resources on these higher‑growth, technology‑intensive areas, the company aims to deepen its role in shaping how pharmaceutical R&D is designed, analyzed and brought through regulatory review worldwide.

The latest update is out from Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies ( (WAB) ).

On April 22, 2026, Wabtec reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, with sales up 13% year over year to $2.95 billion, driven by both its Freight and Transit segments and contributions from recent acquisitions. GAAP diluted EPS rose 12.8% to $2.12 and adjusted EPS increased 18.9% to $2.71, supported by higher gross margins and non-operational benefits, while cash from operations reached $199 million.

Freight segment revenue climbed 11.3% on sharply higher locomotive equipment deliveries and strong digital sales, even as services declined as expected due to lower modernization activity. Transit segment sales grew 17.8% with notable margin expansion, and Wabtec’s multi‑year backlog increased to $30.8 billion with 12‑month backlog up 12.8%, underscoring robust demand and reinforcing management’s view that the company is well positioned for continued profitable growth and value creation for shareholders.

Everest Consolidator Acquisition ( (EVCO) ) has shared an update.

On April 14, 2026, Everest Consolidator Acquisition Corporation held a stockholder meeting at which 94.67% of its 4,876,004 outstanding shares were represented, and investors approved amendments to both the company’s certificate of incorporation and its Investment Management Trust Agreement. These changes, finalized between April 20 and April 21, 2026, permit the company to withdraw interest earned on the trust account on or after January 20, 2026 to cover permitted expenses, and notably, no Class A shares were redeemed in connection with the vote, signaling broad shareholder support and preserving the full cash pool in the trust for a future business combination.

More about Everest Consolidator Acquisition

Everest Consolidator Acquisition Corporation is a special purpose acquisition company that raises capital through an initial public offering to pursue merger or acquisition opportunities. It maintains investor funds in a trust account until a suitable business combination is completed, and corporate actions often focus on managing this trust structure and related shareholder approvals.

Spruce Biosciences ( (SPRB) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 20, 2026, Spruce Biosciences entered into an underwriting agreement with a syndicate led by Leerink Partners, Guggenheim Securities and Oppenheimer to sell 1,150,000 shares of common stock at $50.00 per share and 50,000 pre-funded warrants at $49.99 each. All securities are being sold by the company under an effective shelf registration, and the pre-funded warrants are immediately exercisable subject to ownership caps designed to limit any holder’s beneficial stake in the company.

The underwriters exercised in full a 30-day option on April 21, 2026 to purchase an additional 180,000 shares at the public offering price, bringing expected gross proceeds to approximately $69 million before fees, with closing anticipated on April 22, 2026. This equity raise meaningfully bolsters Spruce’s balance sheet as it advances late-stage neurological programs, though it also dilutes existing shareholders while broadening the company’s institutional investor base.
